meta.title: Biei Town Ski Area
meta.description: A snow wellness hub for people who don't ski

hero.title:
Biei Town
Ski Area
hero.tagline: A snow wellness hub for people who don't ski

transit.title:
From the station,
walk into snow.
transit.lead: About a 15-minute walk from JR Biei Station. Even without a rental car, independent FIT travelers can touch snow directly—a micro-tourism base you won't find at suburban resorts.
transit.imgAlt: Traveler walking white hills—the stroll route from Biei Station to the ski area

primary.title: Snow wellness hub
primary.lead: A space to touch, feel, and restore body and mind—not centered on downhill skiing. Snowshoe meditation walks, snowmen, sledding—a cocooning wellness sanctuary without mega-resort noise.
primary.imgAlt: Snowshoeing an empty snowfield—a snow wellness experience

secondary.title: Into a welcoming space
secondary.lead: Trespassing on farmland is strictly forbidden. Here, you can dive into deep white snow, lie down, and play freely—from "no entry" to "welcome." A outlet for dispersing overtourism.

recovery.title:
After the snow,
warm the body
recovery.lead: Consommé and pot-au-feu simmered for hours with Biei vegetables—local food therapy for chilled bodies. Pair with nearby baths like Shirogane Onsen to complete a holistic wellness cycle.
recovery.whisper: From Okiraku-tei to steaming drinks at station cafés—taste local warmth between strolls.
recovery.imgAlt: Biei vegetable pot-au-feu—a warming post-ski menu

journey.title:
A full day
starting at the station
journey.lead: A customer journey where FIT travelers explore snow play, culture, and views independently after arriving at Biei Station.

01.title: Arrival & intro
01.body: Biei Station. Cold-weather rentals, coffee to-go at ARBOLITO and more

02.title: Immersion
02.body: Free snow play plaza (15-min walk) or a guided hill snowshoe tour

04.title: Culture & reflection
04.body: Art walks at Takuma-kan and Birch Tree Corridor

05.title: Views & finale
05.body: Shirogane Blue Pond winter light-up—the cobalt blue the world knows

wellness.title: Snow wellness experiences
wellness.body: Gateway to official programs like the Furari Winter Walk hill snowshoe tour and Tokotoko Adventure—plus a one-day model pairing free play at the town ski area.
wellness.thumbAlt: Snowshoeing Biei snowfields—a snow wellness experience

walk.title: Micro-tourism on foot from the station
walk.body: Turn the 15 minutes from station to ski area into a "promenade to read Biei winter." GPS audio guides, hands-free rentals, café hopping—an FIT loop without a rental car.

responsible.title: The landscape is someone's livelihood
responsible.body: The landscape is someone's livelihood. No entry to farmland or private property. How to shoot minimalist photos in welcoming spaces. Enjoying Biei as a responsible traveler.

bluePond.title: Shirogane Blue Pond — winter light-up
bluePond.body: The cobalt pond that became a Mac wallpaper. After snow wellness, head to the Shirogane onsen area—the icon overseas visitors search for most in Biei.
bluePond.thumbAlt: Shirogane Blue Pond under winter light-up—cobalt water and snowfields

featuredSpot.title: Shirogane Blue Pond
featuredSpot.lead: The cobalt blue the world knows—close a snow wellness day with a winter light-up finale.
featuredSpot.imgAlt: Shirogane Blue Pond at night under light-up—birch trees and cobalt water

meta.title: Shirogane Blue Pond — Biei Town Ski Area
meta.description: Winter light-up, access, and pairing with snow play—guide for international visitors

hero.title:
Japan's Blue Pond—
beyond the photo
hero.tagline: The cobalt blue the world knows—after snow wellness, into the winter night

bluePond.notice: Light-up dates, parking, and bus service change by season. Confirm on Biei Town or tourism association official sites before you go.
bluePond.introLead: Shirogane Blue Pond (Aoiike) was created as a by-product of erosion-control work linked to Mt. Tokachi volcanic activity.
bluePond.introBody: Minerals from submerged birch and fallen leaves scatter light into cobalt blue—a Biei view where nature and human intervention overlap.
bluePond.whenLead: Typically from around November through April, lights come on after sunset. In crisp midwinter air, the blue sinks deep into still water—the hour overseas visitors seek most.
bluePond.whenNote: Viewing decks or parking may be restricted due to weather, snow, or crowds.
bluePond.accessTitle: From Biei Station & the ski area
bluePond.accessLead: About 25 minutes by car from Biei Station (Shirogane Onsen area). After the ski hill, most visitors go by rental car or winter bus/tour.

drive.label: Car
drive.body: Biei Station → Shirogane Onsen area, approx. 25 min. Winter tires required.

bus.label: Bus & tours
bus.body: Seasonal buses and day tours from Biei/Furano (schedules vary by year).

pair.label: Itinerary
pair.body: Ski area → warming lunch → Shirogane Onsen → Blue Pond light-up in one day.

winter.label: Winter
winter.body: Watch for ice and blizzards. Tripods only in designated areas.

bluePond.responsibleLead: No trespassing on farmland—the same stance as our "landscape is livelihood" message. Shoot from the viewing deck, yield in crowds, and respect tripod etiquette.
bluePond.mapsBody: Shirogane, Biei-cho, Kamikawa-gun, Hokkaido. Check your route on Google Maps.
bluePond.heroImageAlt: Shirogane Blue Pond at night under light-up—birch silhouettes and cobalt water
bluePond.galleryAlt: Winter at Shirogane Blue Pond—cobalt surface framed by snow

meta.title: Snow Play Experiences — Biei Town Ski Area
meta.description: Furari Winter Walk snowshoe tour, Tokotoko Adventure booking, and free snow play—official links included

hero.title:
Snow play
without skiing
hero.tagline: From a free snow plaza to guided hill snowshoes—touch Biei winter

snowPlay.notice: Seasons, prices, and capacity change—each operator's official site is authoritative. Hill access may require a licensed guide (no trespass on farmland or private land).
snowPlay.introLead: Biei Town Ski Area offers free snow play. Go further with the tourism association's hill tour or local operator bookings.
snowPlay.introBody: Enjoy only in welcoming spaces—no entering patchwork farmland. Choose designated routes and guided experiences.
snowPlay.hubTitle: Town ski area snow play plaza
snowPlay.hubLead: About a 15-minute walk from JR Biei Station. Sledding, snowmen, deep-snow play—a wellness-oriented free zone (lift free).
snowPlay.experiencesLead: For hill views and adventures, check the latest info and book on the official pages below.

furari.title: "Furari Winter Walk"
furari.subtitle: Scenic hill snowshoe in Biei
furari.lead: Crisp air and marshmallow hills—a picture-book snowscape on beginner-friendly snowshoes. Meet at Shiki-no-Joho-kan next to JR Biei Station.
furari.season: Typically late December–early March (varies with snow)
furari.meet: Shiki-no-Joho-kan (Biei Tourism Association) · 1 min from JR Biei Station
furari.note: Hill snowshoeing runs only on permitted guided tours. You cannot walk the hills without an authorized guide.

tokotoko.title: Tokotoko Adventure
tokotoko.subtitle: Book Biei winter activities
tokotoko.lead: Snowshoe and snow experiences bookable online. Check availability and courses on the reservation site.

snowPlay.pairingLead: AM: Furari Winter Walk → Lunch near the station → PM: free snow play at the town ski area → Night: Shirogane Blue Pond light-up.
snowPlay.heroImageAlt: Snowshoeing Biei snowfields—a snow wellness experience
snowPlay.galleryAlt: White hills and clear winter sky—Biei snow wellness scenery

access.note: About a 15-minute walk (approx. 1.5 km) from JR Furano Line Biei Station
access.places: 北海道Hokkaido上川郡Kamikawa-gun美瑛町Biei-cho字大村村山Omura-Murayama
